title: Risk Card v1
description: One JSON per project risk. A standalone risk register entry, grounded in the PMI Practice Standard for Project Risk Management and ISO 31000: a risk is assessed (probability x impact), given a response strategy and an owner, and worked down through mitigation action-cards. Use risk-cards when a project needs real risk management; the project-card's inline risks[] remains the lightweight option for small projects. Lives in <project>/risks/.

title.description: Short risk name. Prefer a cause-effect phrasing: 'Vendor sign-off slips, delaying first cutover'.

description.description: The risk in full: the uncertain event and its effect on a project objective.

risk_type.description: A threat is a negative uncertainty; an opportunity is a positive one. Determines how to read the response enum (see response).

impact.description: Severity of effect on project objectives if it materializes.

score.description: DERIVED: P x I on a 1-5 x 1-5 matrix (very-low=1 .. very-high=5), so score should equal the product of the probability and impact levels. Lets you rank the register. Treat as computed from probability/impact, not an independent input — if it contradicts them, the levels win.

response.description: Response strategy. THREATS (PMI): avoid=remove the cause; mitigate=reduce P or I; transfer=shift to a third party (insurance, contract); accept=live with it; escalate=outside project authority. OPPORTUNITIES (risk_type=opportunity): exploit=make it certain; enhance=increase P or I; share=partner to capture it; accept/escalate as above.

status.description: open=identified, not yet being worked; mitigating=response in progress; accepted=consciously living with it; realized=it happened (it is now an issue — consider an issue-card); closed=no longer relevant or fully mitigated.

owner.description: Single accountable owner for managing this risk.

mitigation.description: Summary of the response plan. The concrete steps live as mitigation_action_cards.

mitigation_action_cards.description: Action-card ids that carry out the response. This is how a risk turns into worked-down work.

trigger.description: Early-warning sign that the risk is materializing.

residual.description: Residual risk expected to remain after the planned response.

review_due.description: When this risk should next be re-assessed. A risk nobody has reviewed in months is a register going stale.

realized_as_issue.description: If status=realized: the issue-card it became.

updated.description: Date this card was last touched. Lets tooling sort 'recently changed' across card types.
